schema_notes: concept-level booleans with evidence pointers (must-hit findings). Ground truth lists every orphan, broken wikilink, and tag-drift cluster the audit must surface. The vault contains 52 notes; the audit must surface ALL items in expected_orphans, expected_broken_links, and expected_tag_drift_clusters.

expected_tag_drift_clusters.majority_reason: slash form is the dominant Obsidian-hierarchy variant in the vault (3 files use #project/alpha vs. 1 each for the case- and hyphen-drift variants)
hyphen form is used in 2 files vs. 1 for the underscore variant
hyphen form is used in 2 files (meetings/budget-review.md, meetings/standup-mon.md) vs. 1 underscore (daily-2026-04-08.md)
hyphen form is used in 2 files (daily-2026-04-06.md, meetings/qbr-2026-q1.md) vs. 1 underscore (daily-2026-04-07.md)

the lowercase form is the canonical Obsidian convention; in this vault each variant appears in 1 file (notes/roadmap-draft.md uses #OKR, areas/security.md uses #okr) so either choice is defensible, but #okr matches the convention used by every other lowercase tag

ground_truth.false_positive_traps_pseudo_orphans_reas: Each looks unreferenced at first glance but has at least one explicit backlink: triage-2026-04.md is linked from daily-2026-04-04.md; random-thought.md is linked from areas/customer-onboarding.md; launch-spec-draft.md is linked from projects/alpha.md. These MUST NOT appear in the audit's orphan list.
false_positive_traps_pseudo_broken_links: [[../daily-2026-04-01]] in meetings/launch-postmortem.md (resolves to existing daily-2026-04-01.md via Obsidian relative-path rule)
[[Projects/Alpha]] in references/standards.md (resolves to projects/alpha.md via Obsidian case-insensitive rule)
ground_truth.false_positive_traps_pseudo_broken_links: These wikilinks LOOK broken but resolve under the Obsidian rules documented in obsidian/SKILL.md. They MUST NOT be reported as broken.
ground_truth.rationale: Round 9 (2026-04-30) expanded the vault from ~27 to 52 notes per user feedback to (a) drop the specific note-count from the prompt, (b) make every audit anchor strictly checkable against the vault, and (c) increase the audit surface so executors cannot simply pattern-match on the v8 fixture. The new content is balanced: 6 new genuine orphans (Archive + Drafts + Inbox cleanup candidates), 6 new genuine broken wikilinks (each unresolvable under all three Obsidian wikilink rules), 3 new tag-drift clusters (#follow-up/#follow_up, #weekly-review/#weekly_review, #OKR/#okr), and ~10 well-linked benign notes (people MOC additions, meeting standups, daily notes, dependencies map) that anchor the graph and create enough noise to prevent trivial scans. All three v8 false-positive traps (pseudo-orphans Inbox/triage-2026-04, Inbox/random-thought, Drafts/launch-spec-draft) and both v8 pseudo-broken links ([[../daily-2026-04-01]], [[Projects/Alpha]]) are preserved unchanged so the precision checkpoints continue to bite.
